prompt:
Using REFERENCE_0 as the character and outfit base, create a polished character reference sheet for HANAKO. Keep the face anonymized/blurred as in the reference, but expand the single photo into a full-body studio character sheet.

Goal: Generate a clean, elegant character turnaround sheet with a traditional Japanese cultural theme, preserving the red kimono styling, floral hair ornaments, dangling accessories, and candy skewer prop from the reference.

Canvas and style: Vertical poster layout on a warm off-white paper background with thin muted red divider lines, small ornamental corner marks, serif typography, and a refined fashion/character-design presentation.

Top section: Show exactly 4 full-body turnaround views of the character wearing the same red floral kimono ensemble:
1. Front
2. 3/4 Front
3. Side
4. Back
Add small numbered captions under each view.

Header text: Large title HANAKO with a small cherry blossom icon and the subtitle “CHARACTER REFERENCE SHEET”. Include a compact info block listing: Name: HANAKO, Age: 18, Height: 158 cm, Role: “Traditional Beauty”, Theme: “Japanese Cultural”.

Right sidebar: Add a “DETAILS & CALL-OUTS” panel with exactly 4 labeled close-up cards:
1. Kimono Fabric — subtle woven texture with refined sheen
2. Inner Collar — black & white floral patterned juban
3. Obi — gold floral brocade with traditional knot style
4. Hair Accessory — floral kanzashi with dangling ornaments

Bottom section: Create exactly 3 larger pose panels:
5. Dynamic Bento Pose — standing, lively gesture, holding the colorful candy skewer outward
6. Seated Pose — seated calmly on a wooden bench or platform, holding the candy skewer
7. Expression Pose — closer bust/half-body crop holding the candy near the face

Add a small “COLOR PALETTE” box with exactly 5 circular swatches: deep red, pale pink, blush pink, warm gold, and black. Add a short “NOTES” block describing grace, tradition, refined elegance, and timeless Japanese beauty.

Constraints: Preserve the recognizable clothing, hair accessory placement, and candy prop from the reference while making the sheet look like a professional generated model/character sheet. Do not add extra views or extra callout cards. Keep the face obscured/anonymized in every character view.
